Bank accounts /
Checking accounts /
Teen checking account
For ZIP  

Bank Smartly Checking for teens

Enjoy a $0 monthly maintenance fee,1 contactless debit card and more.

Open an account

TEEN CHECKING ACCOUNT

Independence for them. Peace of mind for you.

Build financial confidence together. Open a U.S. Bank Checking account for your teen that balances freedom and guidance. Teens manage their own debit card while parents stay in the loop.

Open an account Learn about Bank Smartly Checking

$0 Monthly Maintenance Fee

$12 fee is waived for clients under 181

Debit card with contactless payments

Overdraft Fee Forgiveness2 and protection3

Access to over 4,000 fee-free U.S. Bank ATMs45

A debit card that goes anywhere

Debit card: Shop in-store or online, grab food with friends and lock or unlock your card anytime.

Mobile banking: See where your money goes and send cash to friends fast.

Smart Assistant: Get tips on spending and learn how to use your account and debit card.

Manage, guide and grow – together

Stay in control: Easily manage the account via mobile and online banking.

Seamless transfers: Move money quickly from any U.S. Bank or external account.

Shared ownership: Request two debit cards – one for you and one for your teen – while enjoying the benefit of waived Monthly Maintenance Fees.1
 

How to open a teen checking account – it’s quick and easy.

Here’s what you’ll need to get started.

  • Social Security number for both you and your teen
  • A $25 opening deposit made through an internal or external transfer or a credit or debit card
  • Proof of identity for your teen, like a birth certificate or photo ID
Apply online

Here’s what you’ll need to get started.

  • Social Security number for both you and your teen
  • A $25 opening deposit made through an internal or external transfer or a credit or debit card
  • Proof of identity for your teen, like a birth certificate or photo ID
Apply online

Simple resources for building financial knowledge

Explore tips and tools to master banking basics, make the most of digital banking, stay secure and save smarter – all designed for teens taking their first steps in finance.

Start strong

Essentials for your financial journey

Ready for your first checking account or teen debit card? Learn the essentials, like how to deposit checks and avoid overdrafts, to keep your finances on track.

Dive into banking basics

MANAGE WITH EASE

Bank from anywhere

Manage your money anytime, anywhere with the U.S. Bank Mobile App. Check balances, track spending and subscriptions and set up alerts right from your phone.

Explore digital banking tools

STAY SECURE

Smart defense for your digital life

A teen bank account or debit card puts you in control. Our Digital Security Guarantee protects your money, while smart habits keep your info secure.

Strengthen your security

BUILD YOUR FUTURE

Smart habits for serious goals

Open a teen savings account to track goals and boost your balance. Our savings tools make it easy to grow what matters.

Start saving smarter

Frequently asked questions about teen checking accounts

Most banks let teens ages 13 to 17 open a checking account, but it’s usually a joint account with a parent or another adult. The adult helps manage the account by keeping an eye on it, making transfers, deposits and setting spending limits. This gives teens hands-on practice with managing money. When the teen turns 18, they can take full control by opening a new account in their own name.

Your teen can access their money quickly, depending on the type of deposit. Cash deposits and direct deposits are available right away, while other deposits may have holds. They can use their phone to pay, send money or visit an ATM. With the best tools to manage their money, they’ll be ready for anything – whether it’s buying a snack or shopping for clothes online.

You can check balances, set up alerts6 for purchases and create optional spending limits within mobile and online banking. These tools help build trust. Your teen manages their money on their own while you stay in the loop.

This account helps teens build real-world financial skills with tools like a debit card, savings and spending goal trackers and overdraft protection. They can access financial education resources, manage their account easily with the mobile app, and make smarter choices. Parents can set ATM and purchase limits and get alerts6– offering a safe, guided way to learn budgeting and saving. Smart Assistant is available 24/7 for account and debit card questions.

When your teen turns 18, the account can continue to be used as before. While it doesn’t automatically change over, they can work with a banker to explore options for transitioning to an individual account that suits their needs. You can choose to keep or remove parent access, which helps them on their path to managing their own money in the best way possible.

The account works with popular payment apps like Venmo, Zelle®, Apple Pay and Cash App. You can shop safely by tapping your card to pay, using a digital wallet or checking out online with tools like Paze.

Stay protected with powerful security tools designed for your peace of mind. Safeguard your account by locking your card instantly in the app, setting real-time spending alerts6 and relying on fraud protection for unauthorized charges.7 Our Digital Security Guarantee shields your money while you build the smart habits necessary to keep your information secure.

The best choice depends on what the teen needs and what the adult prefers. Full-service banks are a popular option because accounts are safe, user-friendly and offer many helpful features. These include an easy-to-use mobile app with digital tools, spending alerts,6 debit card controls and tools to learn about money. Parents can co-own the account and keep an eye on it, while teens get real-world practice managing money.

Start of disclosure content
Footnote
Return to content, Footnote

Disclosures

  1. Bank Smartly Checking account Monthly Maintenance Fee: For newly opened U.S. Bank Smartly Checking accounts, the Monthly Maintenance Fee is waived for the first 2 statement periods from the account open date. Members of the military (requires self-disclosure), Workplace-Financial Wellness Program, U.S. Bank Global Transition Solutions, clients age 13-24 or clients 65 and over, pay no monthly maintenance fee. State Farm Alliance customers can waive the monthly maintenance fee as an owner of an eligible personal State Farm credit card. All others can have it waived by meeting any one of the following criteria: Have combined monthly direct deposits totaling $1,500 or more; or keep a minimum average account balance of $1,500 or more; or owner on a U.S. Bank Smartly™ Visa Signature® Card; or owner of an eligible U.S. Bank small business checking account; or qualify for one of the three Smart Rewards® tiers (Gold Tier, Platinum Tier or Platinum Plus Tier).

    The average account balance is calculated by adding the balance at the end of each calendar day in the statement period and dividing that sum by the total number of calendar days within the statement period. Other fees may apply.

    Please refer to the Consumer Pricing Information (PDF) disclosure for more details.

  2. U.S. Bank Overdraft Fee Forgiven Program - Consumer checking accounts (excluding Safe Debit accounts) assessed an Overdraft Paid Fee may qualify for a fee waiver. The Overdraft Fee Forgiven period starts the first business day your Available Balance becomes negative and you were charged an Overdraft Paid Fee(s). U.S. Bank will review your account at the end of the Overdraft Fee Forgiven period (11 p.m. ET) and if your Available Balance (excluding the Overdraft Paid Fees and including immediate and same day deposits), is at least $0 we will waive Overdraft Paid Fee(s) charged. Deposits that generally will qualify for Overdraft Fee Forgiven include: ACH and electronic deposits, cash deposits, wire transfers, ATM deposits at U.S. Bank ATMs, check deposits in branch and internal transfers from another U.S. Bank account. Deposits that generally will not qualify for Overdraft Fee Forgiven include: Mobile check deposit, extended hold placed on a deposit and deposits into new accounts opened less than 30 days where funds are generally made available the fifth business day after the day of your deposit. Refer to the Determining the Availability of a Deposit – All Accounts section of Your Deposit Account Agreement (PDF) for full funds availability details.

  3. Overdraft Protection Transfer Fees are waived on U.S. Bank Smartly Checking accounts. If you have linked eligible accounts, and the negative Available Balance in your checking account is $5.01 or more, the advance amount will transfer in multiples of $50. If, however, the negative Available Balance is $5 or less, the amount advanced will be $5. The Overdraft Protection Transfer Fee is waived if the negative Available Balance in your checking account is $50 or less. Please note, if you have Overdraft Protection and your account becomes overdrawn, Overdraft Protection funds will be accessed before the account is eligible for Overdraft Fee Forgiven. No Overdraft Protection Transfer Fee(s) will be charged for transfers from linked deposit accounts.

    Refer to Your Deposit Account Agreement (PDF), section titled Overdraft Protection Plans, for additional information.

  4. ATM Transaction Fee. U.S. Bank will assess this fee for each ATM Transaction conducted at the Non-U.S. Bank ATM. This means that you may be charged more than one ATM Transaction fee at a Non-U.S. Bank ATM during a single ATM session. Non-U.S. Bank ATMs are defined as any ATM that does not display the U.S. Bank logo in any manner, physically on the ATM or digitally on the  screen.

    ATM Surcharge. Non-U.S. Bank ATM owners may apply a surcharge fee on ATM transactions at their ATMs. U.S. Bank participates in MoneyPass®, an ATM surcharge free network. To find MoneyPass ATM locations, select “visit the MoneyPass locator” at the bottom of our ATM locator search results to be taken to the MoneyPass website. If you use an ATM that uses the MoneyPass® Network and are charged a surcharge fee, please call us at 800-USBANKS (872-2657) for a refund of the surcharge fee. However, a Non-U.S. Bank ATM Transaction Fee may be assessed on transactions at a MoneyPass ATM.

    Please refer to the Consumer Pricing Information (PDF) disclosure section titled Miscellaneous Checking, Savings or Money Market Fees for a summary of ATM transaction fees.

  5. When changing checking account types, corresponding Non-U.S. Bank ATM transaction fee waivers will become available on the first day of the next statement cycle. ATM transaction fee waivers are only applicable for your U.S. Bank Smartly® Checking accounts.

  6. For text alerts, standard messaging charges apply through your mobile carrier and message frequency depends on account settings. Please be advised that these alerts may not be sent immediately. Visit Cellular phone contact policy for additional information.

  7. Zero fraud liability – U.S. Bank provides zero fraud liability for unauthorized transactions. Cardholder must notify U.S. Bank promptly of any unauthorized use. Certain conditions and limitations may apply. See the Electronic Fund Transfers section in Your Deposit Account Agreement (PDF)  for details.

Start of disclosure content

Deposit products are offered by U.S. Bank National Association. Member FDIC.

The U.S. Bank Visa® Debit Card is issued by U.S. Bank National Association, pursuant to a license from Visa U.S.A. Inc.
